message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Politbarometer West 1990 (Cumulation, 1st-12th Wave)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.7271","justification":"Contains German public opinion data on political behaviour, government, elections, and economic conditions in 1990. While not limited to individual Länder, respondents include those residing in the requested federal states (e.g. Berlin, Bavaria, North Rhine-Westphalia). Relevant topics: political behaviour and attitudes, government and political systems, economic conditions and indicators."},{"title":"ALLBUS 1990 (German General Social Survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10766","justification":"National representative survey with detailed regional identifiers that allow analysis by state (including Berlin, Bavaria, North Rhine-Westphalia, etc.). Covers social behaviour and attitudes, political attitudes, economic conditions, government and institutions, and some law-and-order topics. Fieldwork in the 1990s (1990)."},{"title":"ALLBUS 1992 (German General Social Survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10767","justification":"Continuation of ALLBUS for the early 1990s, including state identifiers for the listed Länder. Topical coverage includes social attitudes, political behaviour and trust in institutions, evaluations of economic conditions, and attitudes towards government and policy. Suitable for regional comparisons across the specified states."},{"title":"ALLBUS 1994 (German General Social Survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10768","justification":"Mid‑1990s ALLBUS wave with variables on political behaviour, political attitudes, trust in institutions, social attitudes, and assessments of economic conditions and policy. Contains federal state information so analyses can be restricted to Berlin, Baden‑Württemberg, Bavaria, etc."},{"title":"ALLBUS 1996 (German General Social Survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10769","justification":"Covers social behaviour and attitudes, political attitudes, satisfaction with democracy, trust in government, as well as perceptions of economic conditions. Regional identifiers allow sub‑national analysis for the requested Länder. Fieldwork squarely within the 1990s."},{"title":"ALLBUS 1998 (German General Social Survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10770","justification":"Late‑1990s wave of the German General Social Survey with strong coverage of political attitudes, party preferences, evaluations of government and the economic situation, and social value orientations. Federal state identifiers include all requested western German states and Berlin."},{"title":"European Election Study 1994: Voter Study, Germany","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.7742","justification":"German component of the 1994 European Election Study. Focus on electoral behaviour, political attitudes, party choice, and views on European integration and international politics. Includes information enabling regional analysis within Germany, making it suitable for studying electoral behaviour in the specified states during the 1990s."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) – Cumulation of German National Election Studies 1949–2005 (Trend File)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12320","justification":"Trend file cumulating post‑war German federal election studies, including 1990, 1994 and 1998. Contains party choice, political attitudes, evaluations of government and economic conditions, and some foreign‑policy topics. Includes regional identifiers that allow restriction to specific states such as Bavaria, Hesse, or North Rhine‑Westphalia."},{"title":"Crime and Fear of Crime in Germany 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.3802","justification":"Survey on crime, law enforcement, and fear of crime in Germany in the early 1990s. Includes attitudes toward police and criminal justice, perceived security, and related social attitudes. The dataset contains regional information allowing differentiation across German states including those requested."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme (ISSP) 1995: National Identity (Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10484","justification":"German ISSP 1995 module on national identity covers international politics and organisations, conflict/security/peace issues, patriotism, and attitudes toward government and political institutions. Conducted in the 1990s with regional identifiers to analyse respondents in the listed German Länder."}]}
